what_it_is: A TOP that combines images together by adding their pixel values.
mental_model: Stacking light from multiple images on top of each other.
example: Two train videos layered together so brighter parts add up.

Add.meaning: Controls how input images are combined and positioned.

items.Transform Fixed Layer: Keeps one layer fixed while others move.
items.Input to Input One: Adds each input relative to the first input.
items.Pivot: Point used for rotate and scale.

common_notes.Passes: How many times the operation runs.

what_it_is: A TOP that measures image values instead of changing the image.
mental_model: A meter that looks at an image and reports numbers.
example: Measuring how bright the train video is overall or finding the brightest pixel.

Analyze.meaning: Chooses what kind of measurement is done.

Operation.Minimum in RGB: Minimum across red, green, and blue.
Operation.Maximum in RGB: Maximum across red, green, and blue.
Operation.Count Pixels: Counts how many pixels are considered.

Mask.Red: Uses red channel as a mask.
Mask.Green: Uses green channel as a mask.
Mask.Blue: Uses blue channel as a mask.
Mask.Alpha: Uses alpha channel as a mask.

what_it_is: Smooths jagged edges in an image.
mental_model: Softening stair-step edges so lines look smoother.
example: Making diagonal rails in a train video look smooth instead of blocky.

Anti-Alias.meaning: Controls how edge smoothing is done.

Edge Detect Source.RGB: Finds edges based on color changes.

items.Edge Threshold: How strong a change must be to count as an edge.
items.Max Search Steps: How far the algorithm looks for edges.
items.Max Diag Search Steps: How far it looks along diagonals.
items.Output Edges: Shows detected edges instead of the final image.

what_it_is: Finds and tracks moving shapes (blobs) in an image.
mental_model: A tracker that follows moving spots.
example: Tracking people or objects moving across a train camera feed.

Blob Track.meaning: Controls how blobs are detected and tracked.

Mono Source.Luminance: Uses image brightness to find blobs.

items.Blob Bound Color: Color of the drawn blob boxes.
items.Threshold Callback DAT: Runs a DAT when thresholding happens.
items.Mask TOP: Limits blob detection to a masked area.

Constraints.meaning: Rules that decide which blobs are kept or removed.

items.Maximum Move Distance: How far a blob can move between frames.
items.Delete Nearby Blobs: Removes blobs that are too close together.
items.Delete Area Tolerance: How much size change is allowed before deletion.

Revival.meaning: Controls how lost blobs are brought back.

items.Revive Blobs: Allows blobs to reappear after disappearing.
items.Revive Time: How long a blob can be gone before revival.
items.Revive Area Difference: How similar size must be to revive.
items.Revive Distance: How close the new blob must be.

what_it_is: Adds a soft glow around bright parts of an image.
mental_model: Bright light bleeding outward.
example: Headlights on a train glowing softly into the night.

Bloom.meaning: Controls how glow is created and blended.

items.Pre-Black Level: Dark cutoff before bloom is calculated.
items.Bloom Threshold: How bright something must be to glow.
items.Bloom Fill: How much the glow fills outward.
items.Input Bloom: Uses an external image as bloom source.
items.Input Preprocess Bloom: Uses a separate image for pre-bloom processing.

what_it_is: Softens an image by spreading pixel values.
mental_model: Smearing pixels so details become less sharp.
example: Making a moving train look soft and out of focus.

Blur.meaning: Controls how the image is blurred.

Type.Catmull-Rom: Smooth blur with sharper detail than Gaussian.

what_it_is: Stores past images so they can be reused later.
mental_model: A rolling memory of previous frames.
example: Remembering where the train was a few seconds ago.

Cache.meaning: Controls how images are stored and read back.

items.Get One Image on Startup: Stores a single frame when starting.
items.Always Cook: Keeps updating even if not viewed.

what_it_is: Selects a specific image from a Cache TOP.
mental_model: Picking one frame from a stack of saved frames.
example: Choosing exactly which past moment of the train to show.

Cache Select.meaning: Chooses which cached image is output.

items.Cache TOP: Which Cache TOP to read images from.

what_it_is: Remaps image color channels using simple weights.
mental_model: A color mixer with sliders for each channel.
example: Turning a color train video into a custom-tinted version.

Channel Mix.meaning: Controls how output color channels are built.

items.Red: How much red contributes to the output.
items.Green: How much green contributes to the output.
items.Blue: How much blue contributes to the output.
items.Alpha: How transparency contributes to the output.
items.Constant: Adds a fixed value to the output.

what_it_is: Turns numeric channel data into an image.
mental_model: Painting numbers into pixels.
example: Turning train sensor values into a picture you can see.

CHOP to TOP.meaning: Controls how channel data becomes image data.

items.CHOP: The CHOP that provides the numbers.

Data Format.RG: Two channels mapped to red and green.
Data Format.RGB: Three channels mapped to red, green, blue.
Data Format.RGBA: Four channels mapped to red, green, blue, alpha.

items.Clamp CHOP Values: Limits values so they stay in a valid range.

Image Layout.Row per Channel Set: Each channel set becomes one row.
Image Layout.Rows per Channel Set Wrapped: Rows wrap when they reach the edge.
Image Layout.Fit to Square: Arranges data into a square image.

Extra Pixel Values.RGBA: Values used when extra pixels are needed.

what_it_is: Removes a color from an image and turns it into transparency.
mental_model: Cutting out a specific color.
example: Removing a green screen behind a train.

Chroma Key.meaning: Controls which color is removed and how soft the edges are.

items.Hue Minimum: Lowest hue value that is removed.
items.Hue Maximum: Highest hue value that is removed.
items.Hue Soft Low: Soft transition at the low end of the hue range.
items.Hue Soft High: Soft transition at the high end of the hue range.

Output.meaning: Controls how the transparency is applied.

RGB Alpha.Source RGB + New Alpha: Keeps original color with new transparency.

Alpha Output.meaning: Controls which alpha channel is used.

what_it_is: Removes a color from an image and turns it into transparency.
mental_model: Cutting out a specific color.
example: Removing a green screen behind a train.

Chroma Key.meaning: Controls which color is removed and how soft the edges are.

items.Hue Minimum: Lowest hue value that is removed.
items.Hue Maximum: Highest hue value that is removed.
items.Hue Soft Low: Soft transition at the low end of the hue range.
items.Hue Soft High: Soft transition at the high end of the hue range.

Output.meaning: Controls how the transparency is applied.

RGB Alpha.Source RGB + New Alpha: Keeps original color with new transparency.

Alpha Output.meaning: Controls which alpha channel is used.

what_it_is: Combines multiple images using blend modes.
mental_model: A stack of images mixed together with rules.
example: Layering train footage with effects using different blend styles.

Composite.meaning: Controls how multiple TOP inputs are combined.

items.Preview Grid: Shows all inputs in a grid.
items.Select Input: Chooses which input to focus on.
items.Swap Operation Order: Reverses which input is applied first.

Transform.meaning: Controls how inputs are positioned before blending.

what_it_is: Creates a solid color image.
mental_model: A flat sheet of one color.
example: A solid background behind the train video.

Constant.meaning: Defines the color that fills the image.

items.Color / Alpha: The color and transparency of the image.
items.Premultiply RGB by Alpha: Applies transparency directly to the color values.

Output.meaning: Controls how this solid color interacts with an input image.

items.Combine with Input: Uses an input image together with the color.
items.Composite and Set Resolution: Composites color with input and sets output size.
items.Set Resolution Only: Uses the color only to define output size.
items.Operation: How the color combines with the input.
items.Swap Order: Reverses which image is applied first.

what_it_is: Applies a custom filter to an image using a table of numbers.
mental_model: Sliding a small math stencil across an image.
example: Sharpening or embossing details on a train image.

Convolve.meaning: Controls how the custom filter is applied.

items.Coefficients DAT: Table of numbers that defines the filter shape.
items.Apply to Alpha: Also applies the filter to transparency.
items.Offset: Shifts the result brighter or darker.
items.Scale: Multiplies the strength of the filter.

what_it_is: Warps an image by pinning its four corners.
mental_model: Stretching an image by grabbing its corners.
example: Pinning a train video onto a tilted wall or screen.

Extract.meaning: Reads corner positions from an input.

items.Extend: How pixels outside the image are handled.

Pin.meaning: Sets where each corner is placed.

items.Bottom Left: Target position for the bottom-left corner.
items.Bottom Right: Target position for the bottom-right corner.
items.Top Left: Target position for the top-left corner.
items.Top Right: Target position for the top-right corner.
items.Grid Refinement: Adds internal grid points for smoother warping.
items.Background Color: Color used behind the warped image.

what_it_is: Runs a custom C++ plugin to process images.
mental_model: A black-box effect written in C++.
example: A custom-made image effect applied to a train video.

Load.meaning: Controls loading and managing the C++ plugin.

items.Plugin Path: Where the compiled C++ plugin file lives.
items.Reinit Class: Resets and reinitializes the plugin code.

items.Connected Input OPs: List of operators feeding data into the plugin.

what_it_is: Cuts away parts of an image.
mental_model: Trimming the edges of a picture.
example: Cropping the edges of a train video to focus on the center.

Crop.meaning: Defines which parts of the image are kept.

items.Crop Left: Amount removed from the left edge.
items.Crop Right: Amount removed from the right edge.
items.Crop Bottom: Amount removed from the bottom edge.
items.Crop Top: Amount removed from the top edge.

Extend.meaning: Controls how empty areas are filled.

what_it_is: Blends between two images.
mental_model: A fade knob between image A and image B.
example: Fading from one train shot to another.

Cross.meaning: Controls how much of each input is shown.

items.Cross: A slider that mixes between the first and second input.

what_it_is: Blends between two images.
mental_model: A fade knob between image A and image B.
example: Fading from one train shot to another.

Cross.meaning: Controls how much of each input is shown.

items.Cross: A slider that mixes between the first and second input.

what_it_is: Converts images to and from cubemap layouts.
mental_model: Folding and unfolding the six sides of a cube.
example: Wrapping a train environment image around a 3D space.

Cubemap.meaning: Controls how images are converted into cube faces.

Mode.One Input per Side to Cubemap: Uses six separate images to build a cube.
Mode.Vertical Cross to Cubemap: Converts a vertical cross layout into a cube.
Mode.Cubemap to Vertical Cross: Flattens a cube into a vertical cross image.
Mode.Horizontal Cross to Cubemap: Converts a horizontal cross layout into a cube.
Mode.Cubemap to Horizontal Cross: Flattens a cube into a horizontal cross image.

what_it_is: Turns depth information into an image.
mental_model: An image where brightness means distance.
example: Pixels closer to the camera on the train are bright, farther ones are dark.

Depth.meaning: Controls where depth comes from and how it is mapped to pixels.

items.OP: The operator that provides depth data.
items.TOP Layer Index: Which layer is read if the input has layers.
items.Depth Space: Which depth space is used (camera-based depth).
items.Range From: Near distance mapped to one end of the image range.
items.Range To: Far distance mapped to the other end.
items.Clamp To: Limits values so depth stays within range.
items.Gamma: Curves how depth values are distributed.

what_it_is: Shows how two images differ from each other.
mental_model: Subtracting one picture from another to see what changed.
example: Seeing only what changed between two frames of a train video.

Difference.meaning: Computes the pixel-by-pixel difference between inputs.

Transform.meaning: Controls how the two inputs are aligned before comparison.

items.Transform Fixed Layer: Keeps one input fixed while the other moves.
items.Input to Input One: Aligns all inputs relative to the first input.
items.Fill Modes: How inputs are scaled to match size.
items.Pivot: Point used for rotate and scale.

what_it_is: Sends an image directly to a display device.
mental_model: A direct video cable from TouchDesigner to a screen.
example: Sending the train visuals straight to a projector or monitor.

Direct Display Out.meaning: Controls how images are sent to a physical display.

Display.what_it_is: Which screen or output device is used.

Output Color Space.what_it_is: How colors are interpreted when sent to the display.

Output Reference White.what_it_is: Defines how bright pure white is.

items.Minimum Swap Chain Length: How many frames are buffered for display output.
items.Hardware Frame Lock: Locks output timing to display hardware.

what_it_is: Moves pixels of an image using another image as a map.
mental_model: Bending an image based on a height or flow map.
example: Making train footage look wavy using a noise image.

items.Resolution Source: Which input decides the output size.
items.Displace Image: The image that tells pixels where to move.

Horizontal Source.Red: Uses red channel for horizontal movement.
Horizontal Source.Green: Uses green channel for horizontal movement.
Horizontal Source.Blue: Uses blue channel for horizontal movement.
Horizontal Source.Alpha: Uses alpha channel for horizontal movement.

Vertical Source.Red: Uses red channel for vertical movement.
Vertical Source.Green: Uses green channel for vertical movement.
Vertical Source.Blue: Uses blue channel for vertical movement.
Vertical Source.Alpha: Uses alpha channel for vertical movement.

items.Aspect Correct: Keeps movement consistent across aspect ratios.
items.UV Weight: How much UV coordinates are affected.
items.Extend: How pixels outside the image are handled.

what_it_is: Finds and shows edges in an image.
mental_model: Outlining where the image changes sharply.
example: Drawing outlines around the train and tracks.

items.Black Level Strength: How much dark areas affect edge detection.
items.Sample Step: Distance between samples when detecting edges.

Alpha.meaning: Controls how alpha is generated for edges.

Output.meaning: Controls how edges are combined with the image.

Combine with Input.Composite Edge with Input: Draws edges over the original image.

items.Swap Order: Reverses which image is applied first.

what_it_is: Turns edges in an image into a raised or carved look.
mental_model: Making the image look like it’s stamped into metal.
example: Giving the train image a carved, relief-style texture.

Emboss.meaning: Controls how the embossed effect is created.

Select.Luminance: Uses brightness to create the emboss effect.
Select.RGB Average: Uses the average of RGB channels.
Select.RGBA Maximum: Uses the strongest value including alpha.

Method.Use Previous and Next: Compares pixels before and after the current one.
Method.Use Previous and Current: Compares pixels behind the current one.
Method.Use Current and Next: Compares pixels ahead of the current one.

items.Strength: How strong the embossed effect appears.
items.Sample Step: Distance between pixels used for embossing.
items.Direction: Direction the embossing appears to come from.

what_it_is: Feeds a previous frame back into the current frame.
mental_model: Looking at a screen that shows itself from a moment ago.
example: A train image that leaves trails as it moves.

Feedback.meaning: Controls where feedback comes from and when it resets.

items.Target TOP: Which TOP receives the feedback image.
items.Reset: Clears the feedback so it starts fresh.

what_it_is: Resizes and positions an image to fit a target area.
mental_model: Fitting a photo into a frame.
example: Making a train video fit exactly into a screen or panel.

Fit.meaning: Controls how the image is resized and aligned.

Fit Mode.Fit: Resizes the image to fit inside the output area.
Fit Mode.Fit Best: Chooses the best fit without unnecessary scaling.

Fill Direction.Outside: Fills the area completely, may crop.

Justify Horizontal.Right: Aligns the image to the right.

Justify Vertical.Top: Aligns the image to the top.

Transform Order.Translate → Scale → Rotate: Moves first, then resizes, then rotates.
Transform Order.Scale → Rotate → Translate: Resizes first, then rotates, then moves.
Transform Order.Rotate → Scale → Translate: Rotates first, then resizes, then moves.

items.Pivot: Point used for rotate and scale.

what_it_is: Reorients an image by flipping or rotating it.
mental_model: Turning a picture over or changing which side is up.
example: Flipping a train video upside down or mirroring it left to right.

Flip.meaning: Controls how the image is reoriented.

items.Flip X: Mirrors the image left to right.
items.Flip Y: Mirrors the image top to bottom.
items.Bottom Left to Top Left: Moves origin from bottom-left to top-left.
items.Right Back Down: Rotates to a right-facing, downward orientation.
items.Right Front Up: Rotates to a right-facing, upward orientation.
items.Back Up Left: Rotates to a back-facing, upward-left orientation.
items.Front Up Right: Rotates to a front-facing, upward-right orientation.

what_it_is: Applies math functions to image values.
mental_model: A calculator that runs on every pixel.
example: Changing how bright the train image feels using math curves.

Function.meaning: Controls how numeric pixel values are transformed.

items.Rearrange Integers: Reorders numeric values before applying math.
items.Input 1: First value used in the calculation.
items.Input 2: Second value used in the calculation.

Function RGB.meaning: Applies math to the red, green, and blue channels together.

Function RGBA.meaning: Applies math to red, green, blue, and alpha together.

Function A.meaning: Applies math only to the alpha channel.

plain_language_summary.Why it exists: To reshape brightness, contrast, motion, or signal behavior.
plain_language_summary.When to use it: When sliders are not enough and you need math curves.

what_it_is: A place where you write your own GPU code to make images.
mental_model: A tiny robot factory on the graphics card that follows instructions you write.
example: Telling millions of tiny workers how to color and move every pixel of a train image.

GLSL.Version: Which language rules the robot workers use.

Mode.Vertex Shader: Runs on points of shapes before images exist.
Mode.Pixel Shader: Runs on every pixel of the image.
Mode.Compute Shader: Runs general math jobs, not just images.

GLSL.Preprocess Directives: Extra instructions added before the code starts.

Compile Behavior.Stall Until Done: Everything waits until the code is ready.
Compile Behavior.Threaded Show Previous Shader Until Done: Keeps showing the last working image.

Error Behavior.Show Checkerboard: Shows a checkerboard if the code breaks.
Error Behavior.Show Black: Shows nothing if the code breaks.
Error Behavior.Show Previous Shader: Keeps the last image that worked.

Outputs.Load Uniform Names: Finds names in your code automatically.
Outputs.Auto Dispatch Size: Automatically decides how much work to do.
Outputs.Output Access: Decides if the shader can write to the output.

Output Type.3D Texture: A box of pixels with depth.

Outputs.Clear Value: What value is used when erasing.

Inputs.Input Mapping: How inputs are named inside the code.
Inputs.End Value: Fallback number if something is missing.
Inputs.Input Extend Mode UV: What happens at image edges left/right/up/down.
Inputs.Number of Color Buffers: How many images can be written at once.

Colors.Color Name: What the color is called in code.

Matrices.Matrix: A grid of numbers used for transforms.

Buffers.Attribute: Which piece of data to read.
Buffers.Name: What it is called in code.

plain_language_summary.What this really is: You telling the GPU exactly how to think.
plain_language_summary.Core idea: Pixels are numbers, and you decide what math happens to them.

idea_before_starting: Every pixel is just a number. These functions change that number.

functions.Constant Value: Ignore the input and always output the same number.
functions.Square Root: Finds a smaller number that multiplies by itself to make the original number.
functions.Cosine: Like sine, but starts at a different point.
functions.Tangent: Makes a wave that shoots very high very fast.
functions.Arc Sine: Turns a sine value back into an angle.
functions.Arc Cosine: Turns a cosine value back into an angle.
functions.Arc Tangent: Turns a tangent value back into an angle.
functions.Arc Tangent (Input 1, Input 2): Finds the angle based on two numbers, like X and Y position.
functions.Hyperbolic Sine: A curve that grows faster than sine, without looping.
functions.Hyperbolic Cosine: Like hyperbolic sine, but shifted up.
functions.Hyperbolic Tangent: A curve that smoothly goes from -1 to 1.
functions.Log Base 10: Shrinks big numbers using base 10 steps.
functions.Log Base N: Shrinks numbers using a base you choose.
functions.Natural Log (e): Shrinks numbers using a natural growth rule.
functions.Power (Base, Exponent): Multiplies a number by itself many times.
functions.dB to Power: Turns loudness units into raw strength.
functions.Power to dB: Turns raw strength into loudness units.
functions.dB to Amplitude: Turns loudness units into signal size.
functions.Amplitude to dB: Turns signal size into loudness units.

plain_language_summary.Why they exist: To shape motion, brightness, sound, or change speed smoothly.
plain_language_summary.Key idea: Pick a curve, numbers follow it.

what_it_is: A version of GLSL that runs the same shader many times at once.
mental_model: One set of instructions, copied across many outputs.
example: Using one shader to make several different train images at the same time.

how_it_is_different_from_GLSL.short_answer: GLSL runs one shader. GLSL Multi runs many copies of the same shader.

plain_difference.GLSL: One shader → one output image (or set of buffers).
plain_difference.GLSL Multi: One shader → many outputs, all running together.

how_it_is_different_from_GLSL.why_it_exists: To avoid duplicating shader code when you want many similar outputs.
how_it_is_different_from_GLSL.what_problem_it_solves: Running the same shader logic multiple times with different inputs or parameters.

Multi_Behavior.meaning: The shader is evaluated multiple times in parallel.

effects.Number of Color Buffers: Controls how many outputs are produced.
effects.TOPs: Multiple inputs can be mapped per instance.
effects.T Simplex Noise: Built-in noise source available per instance.

explain_like_im_five.GLSL: You tell one robot how to draw one picture.
explain_like_im_five.GLSL_Multi: You tell many robots the same instructions, and they all draw at once.
explain_like_im_five.why_you_would_care: You don’t have to rewrite or duplicate the shader.

Use GLSL Multi when: You want to scale one shader to many cases.

important_note: If you don’t know why you need GLSL Multi, you probably don’t need it yet.

what_it_is: Changes colors by working in hue, saturation, and brightness instead of red, green, and blue.
mental_model: Turning color knobs instead of repainting pixels.
example: Changing the color of a train so it looks warmer, colder, or brighter as it moves.

HSV Adjust.meaning: Pick which colors are affected and how they change.

items.Start Color: The color you start adjusting from. This is the center of attention.
items.Hue Range: How wide of a color slice is affected (how many nearby colors get included).
items.Hue Falloff: How softly the effect fades as colors move away from the start color.
items.Saturation Range: Which saturation levels are affected (dull vs vivid colors).
items.Saturation Falloff: How gently the effect fades across saturation.
items.Value Range: Which brightness levels are affected (dark vs bright).
items.Value Falloff: How smoothly the effect fades across brightness.
items.Hue Offset: Shifts the color around the color wheel.
items.Saturation Multiplier: Makes colors more vivid or more dull.

explain_like_im_five.Hue: What color it is (red, blue, green).
explain_like_im_five.Saturation: How colorful it is (gray vs vivid).
explain_like_im_five.Value: How bright it is (dark vs light).
explain_like_im_five.what_this_operator_does: It finds certain colors and changes them without touching the rest.

what_it_is: Converts colors from HSV format into RGB format.
mental_model: Translating color instructions into something the screen understands.
example: Deciding the train should be 'more blue and brighter' (HSV), then turning that into actual pixel colors (RGB).

HSV to RGB.meaning: Takes hue, saturation, and value and turns them into red, green, blue.

items.Input HSV: The incoming image is treated as Hue, Saturation, and Value instead of RGB.
items.Output RGB: The result is standard RGB pixels that every TOP understands.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It turns color ideas into actual light values.

why_it_exists: HSV is easier for humans to think about.
RGB is what screens actually use.
when_you_use_it: After adjusting colors in HSV space.
Before sending an image to most other TOPs.

what_it_is: Pulls an image from somewhere else in the project and shows it here.
mental_model: A window that looks at another picture without copying it.
example: Watching the train video from another room without moving the train.

Import Select.meaning: Chooses where the image comes from and when it refreshes.

items.Import Parent: Look up to the parent component and grab its image.
items.Texture: Pick exactly which image or texture to pull in.
items.Reload: Re-check the source and update the image.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It lets you see an image from somewhere else without making a new one.
explain_like_im_five.why_thats_useful: If the original changes, this one changes too.
explain_like_im_five.what_it_does_not_do: It does not copy or store the image.

when_you_use_it: You want to reuse an image already made.
You want to keep things connected.
You want updates to flow automatically.

what_it_is: Defines an input slot for a component.
mental_model: A plug socket where images come into a box.
example: A train enters a tunnel. This is the tunnel entrance.

In.meaning: Controls how this input appears and connects.

items.Label: The name shown for this input so humans know what it is.
items.Connect Order: Which input number this is (first, second, third, etc.).

explain_like_im_five.what_this_is: A door for pictures to come in.
explain_like_im_five.label: A sticky note on the door that says what goes through it.
explain_like_im_five.connect_order: Which door is door #1, door #2, and so on.

important_note: This operator does not change images. It only receives them.

what_it_is: Places one image inside another image’s shape or space.
mental_model: Putting a photo inside a frame.
example: The train video appears only inside the shape of a window.

Inside.meaning: Controls how the inside image is fitted and positioned.

items.Fixed Layer: Locks which input is treated as the container.
items.Pre-Fit Overlay: Fits the inside image before placing it.
items.Extend Overlay: Decides what happens when the image goes past the edges.
items.Translate: Moves the inside image left, right, up, or down.
items.Scale: Makes the inside image bigger or smaller.
items.Pivot: Sets the point the image scales or rotates around.
items.Legacy Transform: Uses the older transform behavior instead of the newer one.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It puts one picture inside another picture.
explain_like_im_five.scale: Make the picture bigger or smaller.
explain_like_im_five.pivot: Choose the point it grows from.
explain_like_im_five.extend_overlay: What happens if the picture is too big.

important_note: This operator is about placement, not color or effects.

what_it_is: Brings images and body data from a Kinect sensor into TouchDesigner.
mental_model: A camera that can also see depth and people.
example: The Kinect watches a train platform and knows how far things are and where people are standing.

Kinect.meaning: Controls the Kinect hardware and what data it sends.

items.Active: Turns the Kinect on or off.
items.Hardware Version: Which Kinect model you are using.
items.Image: Turns the camera image on or off.
items.Mirror Image: Flips the image left to right like a mirror.
items.Image Camera Remap: Lines up the color image with the depth view.
items.Too Close Value: What value is used when something is too close to the sensor.
items.Too Far Value: What value is used when something is too far away.
items.Near Depth Mode: Allows closer depth sensing than normal.
items.Sensor: Selects which Kinect device is being used.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_is: A special camera that can tell how far things are.
explain_like_im_five.depth: It knows what is close and what is far.
explain_like_im_five.skeleton: It can guess where people’s arms and legs are.
explain_like_im_five.mirror_image: Makes it act like a bathroom mirror.

important_notes: Some options only work on certain Kinect versions.

what_it_is: Stacks images on top of each other and controls how they line up and combine.
mental_model: Putting photos on a table in layers.
example: A background image of train tracks, with a train image placed on top.

Layer Mix.meaning: Controls what the base layer is and how layers are combined.

items.Background: Chooses what sits behind everything else.
items.Constant Layer: Uses a flat color as the background.
items.Background TOP: Uses another image as the background.

Size.meaning: Controls how big layers are and how they fit.

items.First Input: Uses the first image’s size as the reference.
items.Background Color: Color used when areas are empty.
items.Enable Crop: Cuts off parts that go outside the frame.
items.Outside: Allows the layer to go beyond the frame.

Transform Controls.meaning: Turns on or off ways to move and adjust layers.

items.Extend: Controls what happens past the edges.

Composite.meaning: Controls how layers are ordered and blended.

items.Back to Front: Back layers drawn first, top layers last.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It stacks pictures and lets you move and size each one.

what_it_is: Arranges many images into a grid.
mental_model: Putting photos into rows and columns on a page.
example: Showing many train camera views at once, neatly lined up.

Layout.meaning: Controls how multiple images are arranged together.

items.TOPs: The list of images to arrange.
items.Scale Resolution: Scales images to match a target size.
items.Align: How images line up inside their grid cells.
items.Fit: How each image fits into its cell (stretch, fit, fill).
items.Max Columns: Maximum number of images per row.
items.Border Color: Color of the lines between images.
items.Border: Thickness of the borders between images.
items.Comp Over Background Color: Places images on top of the background color.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It lines up pictures in a neat grid.
explain_like_im_five.max_columns: How many pictures fit across before starting a new row.
explain_like_im_five.fit: Whether pictures stretch or keep their shape.

what_it_is: Brings hand-tracking data from a Leap Motion sensor into TouchDesigner.
mental_model: A small camera that watches your hands instead of your face.
example: Hands moving in the air above the tracks, and the system knows where every finger is.

Leap Motion.meaning: Controls the Leap Motion hardware and how its data is interpreted.

items.Active: Turns the Leap Motion sensor on or off.
items.API: Chooses which Leap Motion software interface is used.
items.Library Folder: Where the Leap Motion support files live on disk.
items.Camera Flip X: Flips the hand image left to right.
items.Camera Flip Y: Flips the hand image upside down.
items.Image Correction: Fixes distortion so hand positions line up correctly.

HMD Mode.Default On: Assumes the sensor is mounted on a VR headset.
HMD Mode.Off: Assumes the sensor is sitting on a desk.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_is: A tiny camera that sees your hands.
explain_like_im_five.what_it_tracks: Hands, fingers, and motion in the air.
explain_like_im_five.flip_x_flip_y: Turn the picture around if it looks backwards.
explain_like_im_five.HMD_mode: Tells it whether it’s on your head or on a table.

important_notes: HMD mode changes how directions are interpreted.

what_it_is: Fixes or adds camera lens bending effects.
mental_model: Straightening or bending a photo taken through a curved lens.
example: Making train tracks look straight again after being bent by a wide camera lens.

Lens Distortion.meaning: Controls how the image is bent or straightened.

items.Invert Distortion: Switches between fixing distortion or adding it.
items.K1: Main strength of barrel or pincushion bend.
items.Optical Center: The true center of the lens.
items.Focal Lengths: How wide or zoomed the lens is.

explain_like_im_five.invert: Fix the image or break it on purpose.

what_it_is: Remaps brightness and color ranges in an image.
mental_model: Turning knobs that say what counts as dark, mid, and bright.
example: Making the train footage darker at night or boosting contrast so tracks stand out.

Pre.meaning: Controls how incoming values are handled before adjustment.

items.Clamp Input: Limits incoming values so they don’t go wild.
items.Automatic Clamp 0–1: Forces values to stay between black and white.
items.Unclamped: Lets values go below black or above white.

items.Gamma: Changes mid-tones without crushing blacks or whites.
items.Contrast: Increases or decreases difference between dark and light.

Range.meaning: Defines what input values map to output values.

items.Out Low: What black turns into after processing.
items.Out High: What white turns into after processing.

RGBA.meaning: Do range control per color channel.

items.Gamma 2: Extra gamma adjustment at the end.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It decides what counts as black, gray, and white.
explain_like_im_five.range: Tells the picture where darkness starts and light ends.
explain_like_im_five.gamma: Fixes how gray feels without breaking shadows.
explain_like_im_five.step: Turns smooth shading into poster-like blocks.

what_it_is: Restricts, wraps, or reshapes numeric image values so they stay within rules.
mental_model: Guard rails for numbers.
example: Keeping the brightness of the train image from getting too dark or too bright.

Limit.meaning: Controls how values are stopped, wrapped, or bounced.

Limit Type (Minimum).Loop: Wraps values back to the top when they go too low.

Limit Type (Maximum).Loop: Wraps values back to the bottom.

items.Normalize: Rescales values to fit evenly between min and max.

items.Quantize Position: Controls whether snapping happens before or after limiting.

explain_like_im_five.loop: Fall off one side and come back on the other.
explain_like_im_five.quantize: Snap to stairs instead of using a ramp.

what_it_is: Adjusts brightness using only luminance (how light or dark something is), not color.
mental_model: Editing a black-and-white version of the image, then applying it back.
example: Making the bright parts of the train stand out while leaving colors alone.

Pre.meaning: Decides what brightness data is used and how it starts.

items.Source: Which channel or calculation is used to get brightness.
items.Gamma: Adjusts mid-tones without crushing blacks or whites.
items.Contrast: Increases or decreases difference between dark and light.

Range.meaning: Maps input brightness to output brightness.

items.Out Low: What black turns into after processing.
items.Out High: What white turns into after processing.

items.Step Size: How big each brightness step is.

items.Gamma 2.0: Extra gamma tweak at the end.
items.Opacity: How strong the effect is overall.

explain_like_im_five.luma: How light or dark something is.
explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It changes brightness without changing color.
explain_like_im_five.step: Turns smooth shading into stair steps.
explain_like_im_five.range: Decides what counts as dark and bright.

what_it_is: Blurs an image based on how bright or dark it is.
mental_model: Bright parts blur one way, dark parts blur another way.
example: Bright headlights on a train blur more than the dark tracks.

items.Type: The blur style or kernel shape.
items.Kernel Width Channel: Which channel decides how strong the blur is.
items.Black Value: How much blur dark areas get.
items.White Value: How much blur bright areas get.
items.Extend: What happens at the edges (hold, repeat, mirror).

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: Bright parts get blurred differently than dark parts.
explain_like_im_five.why_it_matters: You can make glow-like effects without blurring everything equally.

what_it_is: Uses one image to decide how to read colors from another image.
mental_model: A color dictionary.
example: The brightness of the train image is used to look up new colors from a color strip.

Lookup.meaning: Controls how values from one image are used to pick values from another.

items.Method: How the lookup is performed (how values are read and matched).
items.Index Range: The range of values used to look up colors (for example, dark to bright).
items.Index Channel: Which channel is used as the lookup key.
items.Dark UV: Where dark values point to in the lookup image.
items.Light UV: Where bright values point to in the lookup image.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: One picture tells another picture what colors to use.
explain_like_im_five.index_channel: The number used to point at a color.

important_note: This operator replaces colors based on values, not position.

what_it_is: Does math to the numbers inside an image.
mental_model: A calculator that works on every pixel.
example: Changing how bright the train image is by adding, multiplying, or reshaping its numbers.

Fix / Safety.meaning: Decides how to handle broken or weird numbers.

items.Invalid Values: What to do with bad numbers.

Color Space.meaning: Controls how color math is interpreted.

Channel Pre-Op.meaning: Math done to each channel before combining.

Combine Channels.meaning: How R, G, B, A are mixed together.

Channel Routing.meaning: Which channels are used in and out.

Range.meaning: Remaps numbers from one range to another.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: It changes the numbers that make the picture.
explain_like_im_five.why_numbers_matter: Pictures are just grids of numbers.
explain_like_im_five.pre_op_vs_post_op: One happens before mixing colors, one after.
explain_like_im_five.range: Tell the picture what numbers mean dark and light.

what_it_is: Uses one image to cut holes in another image.
mental_model: A stencil that decides what parts you can see.
example: The train is only visible where the matte image is bright.

Matte.meaning: Controls how the masking image is used.

items.Switch Inputs: Swaps which image is the picture and which image is the mask.

Matte Channel.Red: Use the red channel as the mask.
Matte Channel.Green: Use the green channel as the mask.
Matte Channel.Blue: Use the blue channel as the mask.
Matte Channel.RGB Average: Average red, green, and blue for the mask.
Matte Channel.RGBA Average: Average red, green, blue, and alpha.

explain_like_im_five.what_this_does: One picture decides where another picture is visible.
explain_like_im_five.switch_inputs: Decides which picture is doing the hiding.

what_it_is: Flips and reflects an image across a line.
mental_model: Looking at the image in a mirror.
example: The train appears reflected as if there is a mirror along the tracks.

items.Pivot: Where the mirror line is placed.

Extend.Mirror: Reflect the image across the edge.

items.Flip X: Flip the image left to right.
items.Flip Y: Flip the image top to bottom.

explain_like_im_five.mirror: Flip the picture like a reflection.
explain_like_im_five.flip_x_flip_y: Turn the picture sideways or upside down.

what_it_is: Turns a color image into a single grayscale image.
mental_model: Turning a color photo into black and white.
example: The train image becomes light or dark only, no color.

Monochrome.meaning: Controls how color turns into brightness.

items.Monochrome: Use a weighted mix of colors to make grayscale.
items.RGB: Use the red, green, and blue channels together.
items.Clamp To: Limits the output so it stays in a valid range.

what_it_is: Bridges CHOP data into image processing.
mental_model: Using numbers to drive pixels.
example: Train speed values from a CHOP affect image brightness.

MOSIS.meaning: Chooses which CHOP provides the data.

items.CHOP Node: The CHOP that supplies numeric values.

what_it_is: Loads video files or image sequences into TouchDesigner.
mental_model: A video player inside the network.
example: Playing a video of a train moving down the tracks.

items.File: The video or image sequence to load.
items.Play Mode: How playback behaves (play once, loop, etc.).
items.Lock to Timeline: Syncs playback to the main timeline.
items.Specify Index: Manually choose which frame to show.
items.Queue: Lines up another clip to play next.

items.Frame Timeout Strategy: What to do if loading stalls.

operators.what_it_is: Loads a projection-mapping configuration file.
operators.mental_model: A map that tells projectors where to put pixels.
operators.example: Making a train image line up perfectly across multiple projectors.

menu.Configuration File: The MPCDI file that describes the projector layout.
menu.Reload Pulse: Reloads the file if it changes.
menu.Output by Single Region: Outputs one region at a time instead of all.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s a blueprint for where pictures should land in the real world.

operators.what_it_is: Multiplies images together pixel by pixel.
operators.mental_model: Darkening one picture using another picture.
operators.example: A shadow image darkens the train footage.

menu.Fixed Layer: Locks which input is treated as the base.
menu.Fit Overlay: Fits one image onto the other.
menu.Pivot: Point the image rotates or scales around.

operators.explain_like_im_five: White shows everything, black hides everything.

operators.what_it_is: Brings camera-tracking data into TouchDesigner.
operators.mental_model: A camera that knows where it is in space.
operators.example: The virtual train camera moves exactly like a real camera.

menu.Active: Turns NCam tracking on or off.
menu.NCam CHOP: The CHOP that provides tracking data.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells the computer where the camera is and how it moves.

operators.what_it_is: Receives live video over the network.
operators.mental_model: A live video cable over Ethernet.
operators.example: Receiving a live train camera feed from another computer.

menu.Active: Turns the NDI stream on or off.
menu.Search IPs: Where to look for NDI sources.
menu.Bandwidth: How much network data to use.
menu.Hardware Decode: Uses GPU to decode video if available.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s like watching another computer’s screen in real time.

operators.what_it_is: Generates procedural textures made from math instead of images.
operators.mental_model: A pattern generator.
operators.example: Making moving fog or static around the train.

Type.Simplex: Like Perlin but smoother and faster.

menu.Harmonics: How many detail layers are stacked.
menu.Harmonic Spread: How far apart detail layers are.
menu.Harmonic Gain: How strong each detail layer is.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes patterns out of math instead of pictures.

operators.what_it_is: Turns height information into surface direction data.
operators.mental_model: Fake bumps using colors.
operators.example: Making the train tracks look bumpy without adding geometry.

menu.Sample Step: How far apart samples are taken.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes flat pictures look bumpy.

operators.what_it_is: Plays back and manages cached GPU image data.
operators.mental_model: A GPU video recorder/player.
operators.example: Recording frames of a train effect and replaying them.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It remembers pictures on the graphics card.

operators.what_it_is: Passes an image through unchanged.
operators.mental_model: A wire.
operators.example: Letting the train image pass through untouched.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It does nothing, on purpose.

operators.what_it_is: Selects and outputs data from an OpenColorIO device or stream.
operators.mental_model: Picking one color stream out of many.
operators.example: Choosing one color-corrected train feed from several options.

menu.Active: Turns the operator on or off.
menu.OCDevice CHOP: CHOP that provides OpenColorIO device data.
menu.Cache Size: How much data is stored for reuse.
menu.Output Index: Which item in the stream to output.
menu.Output Data Format: How the output data is structured.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It chooses which color data you want to look at.

operators.what_it_is: Applies professional color-management rules.
operators.mental_model: A translator between different color worlds.
operators.example: Making the train video look correct on different screens.

File Transform.Use File Information: Reads color data from the file.

CDL Transform.CDL Mode: Which color correction style to use.

OP Viewer.Operator Viewer: Shows the result in the viewer.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes sure colors look right everywhere.

operators.what_it_is: Outputs images to VR headsets.
operators.mental_model: A window into virtual reality.
operators.example: Seeing the train scene inside a VR headset.

menu.Active: Turns VR output on or off.
menu.Output Color Space: Color format sent to the headset.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends pictures to VR goggles.

operators.what_it_is: Detects motion between frames.
operators.mental_model: Seeing which way pixels are moving.
operators.example: Detecting how fast the train is moving across the screen.

menu.Grid Size: How detailed the motion grid is.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It watches how pixels move from frame to frame.

operators.what_it_is: Selects different kinds of data coming from an Orbex sensor.
operators.mental_model: Choosing which camera view you want from one device.
operators.example: Picking the depth view instead of the color view of a train scene.

menu.Active: Turns the output on or off.
menu.Orbex TOP: The Orbex source this operator reads from.

operators.explain_like_im_five: One sensor can see many things. This lets you choose which one you want.

operators.what_it_is: Connects to an Auster LiDAR device and brings its data into TouchDesigner.
operators.mental_model: A laser scanner that sees the world in dots.
operators.example: Scanning a train platform with lasers to get a 3D picture.

menu.Active: Turns the device on or off.
menu.Device Address: IP address of the Auster device.
menu.IMU Port: Network port for motion sensor data.
menu.Red: Maps data to the red channel.
menu.Green: Maps data to the green channel.
menu.Blue: Maps data to the blue channel.

operators.note: There is also a Timing menu, but it was not visible.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It shoots lasers, listens for the bounce, and draws dots where things are.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses which data from an Auster scan to output.
operators.mental_model: Picking which part of the laser scan you want to see.
operators.example: Using only the depth information from a LiDAR scan of a train.

menu.Auster TOP: The Auster source to read from.

operators.explain_like_im_five: The laser gives lots of numbers. This picks which ones become a picture.

operators.what_it_is: Defines an output slot for a component.
operators.mental_model: An exit door for images.
operators.example: Sending the processed train image out of a component.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It says which picture leaves the box, and through which door.

operators.what_it_is: Selects different kinds of data coming from an Orbex sensor.
operators.mental_model: Choosing which camera view you want from one device.
operators.example: Picking the depth view instead of the color view of a train scene.

menu.Active: Turns the output on or off.
menu.Orbex TOP: The Orbex source this operator reads from.

operators.explain_like_im_five: One sensor can see many things. This lets you choose which one you want.

operators.what_it_is: Connects to an Auster LiDAR device and brings its data into TouchDesigner.
operators.mental_model: A laser scanner that sees the world in dots.
operators.example: Scanning a train platform with lasers to get a 3D picture.

menu.Active: Turns the device on or off.
menu.Device Address: IP address of the Auster device.
menu.IMU Port: Network port for motion sensor data.
menu.Red: Maps data to the red channel.
menu.Green: Maps data to the green channel.
menu.Blue: Maps data to the blue channel.

operators.note: There is also a Timing menu, but it was not visible.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It shoots lasers, listens for the bounce, and draws dots where things are.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses which data from an Auster scan to output.
operators.mental_model: Picking which part of the laser scan you want to see.
operators.example: Using only the depth information from a LiDAR scan of a train.

menu.Auster TOP: The Auster source to read from.

operators.explain_like_im_five: The laser gives lots of numbers. This picks which ones become a picture.

operators.what_it_is: Defines an output slot for a component.
operators.mental_model: An exit door for images.
operators.example: Sending the processed train image out of a component.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It says which picture leaves the box, and through which door.

operators.what_it_is: Shows everything except the overlapping area of two images.
operators.mental_model: Everything outside the frame stays visible.
operators.example: Only the area outside the train silhouette shows.

menu.Fixed Layer: Locks which input is the reference.

operators.explain_like_im_five: You see everything except where the pictures overlap.

operators.what_it_is: Places one image on top of another.
operators.mental_model: Stacking photos.
operators.example: The train sits on top of the background.

menu.Fixed Layer: Choose which input is on top.

operators.explain_like_im_five: One picture goes on top of another.

operators.what_it_is: Groups images into a packed format.
operators.mental_model: Putting items into a box.
operators.example: Bundling several train images together.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It packs pictures together without changing them.

operators.what_it_is: Connects to Adobe Photoshop and pulls images live.
operators.mental_model: A live window into Photoshop.
operators.example: Editing the train image in Photoshop and seeing updates instantly.

menu.Active: Turns the connection on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It watches Photoshop and copies the picture into TouchDesigner.

operators.what_it_is: Loads point-based image data from a file.
operators.mental_model: Reading dots from a file and turning them into pixels.
operators.example: Loading point data that draws the outline of a train.

Tune.Frame Timeout Strategy: What to do if loading stalls.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It reads dots from a file and turns them into a picture.

operators.what_it_is: Selects color data from a Point File In TOP.
operators.mental_model: Picking which color values the dots should use.
operators.example: Choosing how point data colors a train outline.
operators.explain_like_im_five: The dots have numbers. This decides which numbers become colors.

operators.what_it_is: Moves or rotates point-based image data.
operators.mental_model: Moving dots around in space.
operators.example: Rotating or scaling a point-based train image.

menu.Transform TOP: Optional TOP used as a transform source.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It moves and turns the dots.

operators.what_it_is: Controls how strongly transforms affect points.
operators.mental_model: Some dots move more than others.
operators.explain_like_im_five: Some dots listen more than others.

operators.what_it_is: Lines up point data with something else.
operators.mental_model: Snapping dots into place.
operators.example: Aligning point data to a 3D model of a train.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It helps dots line up with something else.

operators.what_it_is: Turns particle (POP) data into an image.
operators.mental_model: Drawing particles as pixels.
operators.example: Showing particle motion around a train.

POP Mode.Position and Active: Use particle position and on/off state.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns moving dots into a picture.

operators.what_it_is: Prepares environment maps for lighting.
operators.mental_model: Getting a light map ready before use.
operators.example: Preparing sky lighting for a train scene.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It gets light maps ready for lighting.

operators.what_it_is: Converts images between projection formats.
operators.mental_model: Changing how a picture wraps around space.
operators.example: Turning a flat train panorama into a dome projection.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes how a picture is wrapped around a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a smooth transition between values or colors.
operators.mental_model: A gradient strip.
operators.example: The sky behind the train slowly fades from dark to light.

menu.DAT: Optional DAT that defines ramp points.
menu.Type: Shape of the ramp (linear, radial, etc.).
menu.Extend Left: What happens before the ramp starts.
menu.Extend Right: What happens after the ramp ends.
menu.Dither: Adds tiny noise to reduce banding.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes smooth color fades.

operators.what_it_is: Brings depth-camera data from Intel RealSense devices into TouchDesigner.
operators.mental_model: A camera that sees distance.
operators.example: Knowing how far the train is from the camera.

setup.Active: Turns the camera on or off.
setup.Mirror Image: Flips the image like a mirror.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s a camera that knows how far things are.

operators.what_it_is: Draws a rectangle shape.
operators.mental_model: A colored box.
operators.example: A flat sign behind the train.

menu.Size: Width and height of the rectangle.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It draws a box you can color and move.

operators.what_it_is: Moves pixels around based on other images or rules.
operators.mental_model: A map that tells each pixel where to go.
operators.example: The train image bends as if viewed through warped glass.

menu.Depth Resolution Source: Where depth size comes from (if used).
menu.Extend: What happens when pixels go past edges.

operators.explain_like_im_five: Each pixel asks a map where it should move.

operators.what_it_is: Draws 3D scenes into a 2D image.
operators.mental_model: A camera taking a picture of 3D stuff.
operators.example: Rendering a 3D train scene into an image.

Advanced.Render Dither: Adds tiny noise to reduce banding.
Advanced.Number of Color Buffers: How many color outputs are made.
Advanced.Overdraw Limit: Limits how much overlapping draw happens.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It takes a picture of a 3D world.

operators.what_it_is: Extra inputs used by the Render TOP.

menu.Crop: Cuts the rendered image to a region.

operators.explain_like_im_five: Extra knobs that help rendering work.

operators.what_it_is: Renders a 3D scene in multiple steps instead of all at once.
operators.mental_model: Taking several photos of the same scene, each for a different purpose.
operators.example: One pass for the train color, another for depth, another for lighting.

Render.Render: Turns this render pass on or off.
Render.Render Pass TOP: Where this pass sends its result.

Advanced.Render Dither: Adds tiny noise to avoid color banding.
Advanced.Overdraw Limit: Cap how much overlap is allowed.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It splits drawing into layers so each job is easier.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses a specific output from a Render or Render Pass.
operators.mental_model: Picking one photo out of a stack.
operators.example: Selecting just the depth image from a train render.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks which render result you want.

operators.what_it_is: A simplified 3D renderer with fewer controls.
operators.mental_model: A point-and-shoot camera for 3D.
operators.example: Quickly rendering a simple 3D train without setup.

menu.Orthographic FOV: Field of view for ortho view.
menu.Camera Distance: How far the camera is from objects.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It draws 3D fast, without many knobs.

operators.what_it_is: Receives rendered images from another process or machine.
operators.mental_model: A live render feed coming in.
operators.example: Receiving a train render from another computer.

menu.Active: Turns the stream on or off.
menu.Name: Which stream to receive.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens for a render being sent to you.

operators.what_it_is: Sends rendered images to another process or machine.
operators.mental_model: Broadcasting a render.
operators.example: Sending the train render to another system.

menu.Active: Turns streaming on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends your picture somewhere else.

operators.what_it_is: Reassigns which input channel goes to each output channel.
operators.mental_model: Rewiring which pipes feed red, green, blue, and alpha.
operators.example: Swap channels so the train image's red comes from input 2 instead of input 1.

menu.Output Red: Choose Input 1, Input 2, Input 3, or Input 4 for the red output.
menu.Output Green: Choose Input 1, Input 2, Input 3, or Input 4 for the green output.
menu.Output Blue: Choose Input 1, Input 2, Input 3, or Input 4 for the blue output.
menu.Output Alpha: Choose Input 1, Input 2, Input 3, or Input 4 for the alpha output.

operators.explain_like_im_five: You decide which input becomes red, green, blue, and see-through.

operators.what_it_is: Controls how resizing is done.
operators.mental_model: A resize switch: careful vs fast.
operators.example: Resize the train image with better quality.
operators.explain_like_im_five: On looks nicer. Off is faster.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a mask by keeping or removing pixels based on their RGB values.
operators.mental_model: A color bouncer that decides which pixels stay.
operators.example: Remove a green screen behind the train by keying out green ranges.

Red.Soft Low: Soft edge on the low side of red.
Red.Soft High: Soft edge on the high side of red.

Green.Soft Low: Soft edge on the low side of green.
Green.Soft High: Soft edge on the high side of green.

Blue.Soft Low: Soft edge on the low side of blue.
Blue.Soft High: Soft edge on the high side of blue.

Output.Invert New Alpha: Flips the mask so kept becomes removed and removed becomes kept.

operators.explain_like_im_five: You set color ranges you want, and everything else gets cut out.

operators.what_it_is: Converts RGB color into HSV color.
operators.mental_model: Changing the way the same color is described.
operators.example: Turn the train image into HSV so you can adjust hue more easily.
operators.explain_like_im_five: Same colors, different labels.

operators.what_it_is: Uses a configuration file to map an image to a scalable display setup.
operators.mental_model: A blueprint for putting one picture across one or more displays.
operators.example: Make the train image line up across multiple screens.

Setup.Configuration File: File that defines the display mapping.
Setup.Apply Camera Offset: Applies an offset to the camera.
Setup.Input Resolution Source: Where the input resolution is taken from.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It helps one picture fit correctly on a big or multi-screen setup.

operators.what_it_is: Places an image onto the screen with basic positioning controls.
operators.mental_model: Laying a photo onto a table and adjusting how it sits.
operators.example: Rotate and scale a train image so it fits the screen.

Transform.Fixed Layer: Locks which input layer is transformed.
Transform.Pre-Fit Overlay: Fits the image before other transforms happen.
Transform.Extend Overlay: Decides what happens outside the image edges.
Transform.Pivot: Sets the point the image rotates and scales around.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you move, turn, and resize the picture on the screen.

operators.what_it_is: Captures pixels directly from a display or window.
operators.mental_model: Taking a screenshot over and over.
operators.example: Grab the desktop showing a moving train video.

menu.Active: Turns screen grabbing on or off.
menu.Source: Which screen or window to grab.
menu.Limit FPS: Caps how often the screen is captured.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It copies what’s on your screen into TouchDesigner.

operators.what_it_is: Runs Python code that controls how the TOP behaves.
operators.mental_model: A little robot following written instructions.
operators.example: Change the train image using custom code.

menu.Callbacks: Functions that run on specific events.
menu.Modify Outside of Cook: Allows changes without forcing a full update.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you control the picture using code.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses one TOP to pass through.
operators.mental_model: Picking one picture from many.
operators.example: Select the train image from several inputs.

menu.TOP: The TOP that gets passed through.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It chooses which picture you want.

operators.what_it_is: Receives images from shared memory.
operators.mental_model: Reading a picture from a shared clipboard.
operators.example: Receive a train image sent from another app.

menu.Shared Mem Name: Name of the shared memory block.
menu.TOP SHM: Which shared memory object to use.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It takes a picture that another program shared with you.

operators.what_it_is: Sends images into shared memory.
operators.mental_model: Putting a picture onto a shared clipboard.
operators.example: Send the train image to another program.

menu.Active: Turns sharing on or off.
menu.Shared Mem Name: Name of the shared memory block.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shares your picture so other programs can use it.

operators.what_it_is: Receives image or data streams from a SICK sensor device.
operators.mental_model: A live camera feed coming from a special machine sensor.
operators.example: Getting a depth or intensity image of a train from a SICK scanner.

menu.Active: Turns the device connection on or off.
menu.Launch File: File used to start or configure the device connection.
menu.Device Address: Network address of the SICK device.
menu.Red: Which data goes into the red channel.
menu.Green: Which data goes into the green channel.
menu.Blue: Which data goes into the blue channel.
menu.Alpha: Which data goes into the alpha channel.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens to a special camera machine and turns what it sees into a picture.

operators.what_it_is: Highlights how quickly image values change, like detecting edges or slopes.
operators.mental_model: Feeling how steep a hill is by walking on it.
operators.example: Detect the sharp edges of a train by seeing where brightness changes fast.

menu.Red: Apply slope detection to the red channel.
menu.Green: Apply slope detection to the green channel.
menu.Blue: Apply slope detection to the blue channel.
menu.Alpha: Apply slope detection to the alpha channel.
menu.Zero Point: Value treated as the center or neutral point.
menu.Strength: How strong the slope effect is.
menu.Sample Step: How far apart pixels are checked.
menu.Extend: What happens at the image edges.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows where the picture changes fast, like finding edges.

operators.what_it_is: Converts image data into a frequency-style representation.
operators.mental_model: Turning sound into a music equalizer, but for images.
operators.example: Analyze repeating patterns in a moving train image.

menu.Coordinate System: How the spectrum is laid out.
menu.Transform Rows: How rows are transformed into spectrum data.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows patterns inside the picture instead of the picture itself.

operators.what_it_is: Adds soft shadowing to 3D renders based on nearby surfaces.
operators.mental_model: Shadows that appear where objects are close together.
operators.example: Add soft shadows under a train to make it look more real.

menu.Quality: How detailed the shadow calculation is.
menu.Sample Directions: How many directions are checked for shadowing.
menu.Blur Radius: How much the shadow is blurred.
menu.Blur Sharpness: How crisp the blurred shadow edges are.
menu.Combine With Color: Mix shadows back into the color image.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It adds soft shadows so things don’t look flat.

operators.what_it_is: Receives image or data streams from a SICK sensor device.
operators.mental_model: A live camera feed coming from a special machine sensor.
operators.example: Getting a depth or intensity image of a train from a SICK scanner.

menu.Active: Turns the device connection on or off.
menu.Launch File: File used to start or configure the device connection.
menu.Device Address: Network address of the SICK device.
menu.Red: Which data goes into the red channel.
menu.Green: Which data goes into the green channel.
menu.Blue: Which data goes into the blue channel.
menu.Alpha: Which data goes into the alpha channel.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens to a special camera machine and turns what it sees into a picture.

operators.what_it_is: Highlights how quickly image values change, like detecting edges or slopes.
operators.mental_model: Feeling how steep a hill is by walking on it.
operators.example: Detect the sharp edges of a train by seeing where brightness changes fast.

menu.Red: Apply slope detection to the red channel.
menu.Green: Apply slope detection to the green channel.
menu.Blue: Apply slope detection to the blue channel.
menu.Alpha: Apply slope detection to the alpha channel.
menu.Zero Point: Value treated as the center or neutral point.
menu.Strength: How strong the slope effect is.
menu.Sample Step: How far apart pixels are checked.
menu.Extend: What happens at the image edges.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows where the picture changes fast, like finding edges.

operators.what_it_is: Converts image data into a frequency-style representation.
operators.mental_model: Turning sound into a music equalizer, but for images.
operators.example: Analyze repeating patterns in a moving train image.

menu.Coordinate System: How the spectrum is laid out.
menu.Transform Rows: How rows are transformed into spectrum data.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows patterns inside the picture instead of the picture itself.

operators.what_it_is: Adds soft shadowing to 3D renders based on nearby surfaces.
operators.mental_model: Shadows that appear where objects are close together.
operators.example: Add soft shadows under a train to make it look more real.

menu.Quality: How detailed the shadow calculation is.
menu.Sample Directions: How many directions are checked for shadowing.
menu.Blur Radius: How much the shadow is blurred.
menu.Blur Sharpness: How crisp the blurred shadow edges are.
menu.Combine With Color: Mix shadows back into the color image.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It adds soft shadows so things don’t look flat.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses one input image from many, with optional blending.
operators.mental_model: A TV switcher that flips between channels.
operators.example: Switch between different camera views of the train.

menu.Blend Between Inputs: Smoothly blends when switching instead of cutting.
menu.Extend: What happens when the index goes outside the input range.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides which picture you’re looking at right now.

operators.what_it_is: Receives a live image stream from another application.
operators.mental_model: A live video pipe coming from another program.
operators.example: Bring a train video from another app into TouchDesigner.

menu.Use Spout: Choose Spout instead of Syphon (Windows vs macOS style).
menu.Active: Turns receiving on or off.
menu.Sender: Which app or stream to listen to.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens for a picture another app is sharing.

operators.what_it_is: Sends an image to another application in real time.
operators.mental_model: Sharing your screen with another app.
operators.example: Send the train render to a VJ or projection app.

menu.Active: Turns sharing on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shares your picture so other apps can use it.

operators.what_it_is: Renders text as an image.
operators.mental_model: Typing words onto a picture.
operators.example: Put the train name or speed as text on screen.

Text.Field Component: Pull text from a UI field.
Text.DAT: Pull text from a table or script.
Text.Append Value: Add a number to the text.

menu.Font: Font family, size, spacing, and style.
menu.Output: How the text image is output.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns words into a picture.

operators.what_it_is: Stores multiple images as layers inside a single 3D texture.
operators.mental_model: A flipbook where each page is stacked into depth.
operators.example: Store many frames of a train animation inside one texture.

menu.Active: Turns the operator on or off.
menu.Replace Single: Replace only one slice of the texture.
menu.Prefill Cache Size: How many slices are prepared ahead of time.
menu.Step Size: How slices advance through the cache.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It stacks lots of pictures into one deep picture.

operators.what_it_is: Turns parts of an image on or off based on brightness or color.
operators.mental_model: A yes-or-no gate for pixels.
operators.example: Keep only the bright parts of a train image.

menu.Comparator: How values are compared (greater, less, etc.).
menu.Soften: Blurs the hard edge of the threshold.

operators.explain_like_im_five: Pixels brighter than the line stay. Others disappear.

operators.what_it_is: Repeats or rearranges an image across space.
operators.mental_model: Stamp the same picture over and over.
operators.example: Repeat a train texture across the screen.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It copies the same picture many times to fill space.

operators.what_it_is: Samples images over time using a third (W) dimension.
operators.mental_model: Scrubbing backward and forward through time.
operators.example: Look at earlier frames of a moving train.

menu.W Coordinate: Which moment in time is sampled.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you look at pictures from the past.

operators.what_it_is: Converts high-brightness images into viewable brightness ranges.
operators.mental_model: Squeezing very bright light into something your eyes can see.
operators.example: Make a very bright train render look right on a normal screen.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes super-bright pictures comfortable to look at.

operators.what_it_is: Receives image data sent over the network from another TouchDesigner system.
operators.mental_model: A network inbox for pictures.
operators.example: Receive a train image being sent from another computer.

menu.Computer Name: Name or address of the sending computer.
menu.Active: Turns receiving on or off.
menu.Maximum Queue: How many incoming frames can wait in line.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens for pictures coming from another computer.

operators.what_it_is: Sends image data over the network to another TouchDesigner system.
operators.mental_model: A network mailbox that sends pictures.
operators.example: Send the train image to another computer.

menu.FPS: How many frames per second are sent.
menu.Cook Always: Always sends frames, even if nothing changes.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends your picture to another computer.

operators.what_it_is: Moves, rotates, scales, and colors an image.
operators.mental_model: Picking up a photo and moving it around.
operators.example: Rotate and scale the train image and change its background.

menu.Transform Order: The order that move, rotate, and scale happen.
menu.Pivot: Point used for rotate and scale.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you move, turn, and resize the picture.

operators.what_it_is: Places one image underneath another using transform controls.
operators.mental_model: Sliding a picture under another picture.
operators.example: Put one train image behind another.

Transform.Extend Overlay: What happens outside the image edges.
Transform.Pivot: Point used for rotate and scale.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts one picture underneath another.

operators.what_it_is: Brings live video from a physical capture device into TouchDesigner.
operators.mental_model: A camera cable plugged straight into TouchDesigner.
operators.example: Capture a live camera pointed at a moving train.

Video In.Active: Turns video capture on or off.
Video In.Device: Which physical camera or capture card is used.
Video In.Allow Fallback Selection: Automatically choose another device if the main one fails.
Video In.Deinterlace: Converts interlaced video into full frames.
Video In.Signal Format: Format of the incoming video signal.
Video In.Transfer Mode: How video data is transferred internally.
Video In.Specify IP: Use a specific IP address for network devices.
Video In.Field Precedence: Which field comes first in interlaced video.
Video In.Quad Link: Uses multiple cables for high-resolution video.
Video In.Sync Timeout: How long to wait for sync.

Options.Maximum Exposure: Caps how long the sensor gathers light.
Options.Bandwidth Limit: Limits data rate from the device.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets TouchDesigner see what a real camera sees.

operators.what_it_is: Sends video from TouchDesigner to a physical output device.
operators.mental_model: A video cable going out to a monitor or broadcast system.
operators.example: Send the train image to an external display or recorder.

menu.Active: Turns video output on or off.
menu.Device: Which physical output device is used.
menu.Signal Format: Format of the outgoing video signal.
menu.Output Bit Depth: Color precision of the output video.
menu.Quad Link Mode: Uses multiple cables for high-resolution output.
menu.Output Color Format: Color format of the output video.
menu.Audio CHOP: Audio data sent with the video.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends your picture out to a real screen or system.

operators.what_it_is: Applies projector calibration and blending using a VIOSO configuration.
operators.mental_model: A map that tells projectors exactly where to put pixels.
operators.example: Correctly align and blend multiple projectors showing a long train scene.

menu.Output by Projector Index: Outputs one projector’s view at a time.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It helps projectors line up perfectly like puzzle pieces.

operators.what_it_is: Renders a web page or web app into an image.
operators.mental_model: A web browser turned into a picture.
operators.example: Display a live train schedule webpage inside TouchDesigner.

menu.Active: Turns web rendering on or off.
menu.Source: URL or local file to load.
menu.Only Update When Loaded: Updates only after page finishes loading.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns a website into a live picture.

operators.what_it_is: Receives video and depth data from a ZED stereo camera.
operators.mental_model: A camera that sees depth, not just color.
operators.example: Capture a train with real depth information.

menu.Active: Turns the camera on or off.
menu.Too Close Value: Value used when objects are too close.
menu.Too Far Value: Value used when objects are too far.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s a camera that knows how far away things are.

operators.what_it_is: Selects specific outputs from a ZED camera feed.
operators.mental_model: Choosing which part of the camera you want to see.
operators.example: Use only the depth image from the train capture.

menu.Active: Turns selection on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks which camera view you want.
